channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Minor Dispute Between Kashmiri and Bihari Students Leads to Clash at Amritsar College
item.description: A disagreement between students from Kashmir and Bihar escalated into a physical clash at the Khalsa College of Engineering and Technology in Amritsar. The incident began in the hostel mess over a min...

item.title: Ginoogaming and Long Lake #58 First Nations declare joint state of emergency over rising drug-related violence
item.description: Two neighbouring First Nations communities in northwestern Ontario, Ginoogaming and Long Lake #58, have jointly declared a state of emergency in response to increasing crime and drug trafficking affec...

item.title: SCA allows authorised non-legal representatives in Tax Court proceedings
item.description: In a landmark decision, the Supreme Court of Appeal (SCA) has ruled that taxpayers in South Africa may be represented in the Tax Court by duly authorised non-legal practitioners, such as accountants, ...

item.title: White House criticised for posting modified Drake album cover with political imagery
item.description: The White House has faced immediate backlash after sharing an altered version of Drake’s newly released album 'Iceman' on social media. The original album cover depicts a hand adorned with a bejewelle...

item.title: Ebola outbreak confirmed in Congo’s Ituri province with 80 deaths
item.description: Health authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo have confirmed a new outbreak of Ebola in the eastern Ituri province, resulting in 80 deaths so far. The Ministry of Health reported 246 suspecte...

item.title: Holly Hunter’s Dedicated Preparation for Her Role in The Piano
item.description: Holly Hunter’s performance as Ada McGrath in the 1993 film 'The Piano' is widely regarded as one of the most remarkable in cinematic history. Known for her meticulous preparation, Hunter, who has been...

item.title: A Look Inside George Oduor’s Home and Memorial in Siaya County
item.description: TikToker Jarunda recently gave Kenyans a peek into the late George Oduor’s home in Asembo, Siaya County. Oduor, who served as the long-time bodyguard for former Prime Minister Raila Odinga, passed awa...

item.title: Law Minister emphasises coalition consensus before any constitutional amendment
item.description: On Saturday, Law Minister Azam Nazeer Tarar clarified that the federal government will not proceed with any constitutional amendment without prior consensus from its coalition partners. Speaking in La...

item.title: Repurposing Abandoned Oil and Gas Wells for Geothermal Energy
item.description: Across the United States, millions of abandoned oil and gas wells continue to leak methane and pollute groundwater, presenting both an environmental challenge and a potential opportunity. States inclu...

item.title: Rediscovering Identity After Retirement: One Woman’s Journey
item.description: After retiring from a long corporate career, Wendy C. Wilson faced an unexpected struggle: she no longer knew who she was without her job. For years, her identity had been tied to her professional rol...

item.title: Look-out circular issued against Bandi Sai Bhageerath in POCSO case as court defers anticipatory bail decision
item.description: The Cyberabad police have issued a look-out circular for Bandi Sai Bhageerath, son of Union minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar, in connection with allegations of sexual harassment of a minor under the Protec...

item.title: Man Found Not Criminally Responsible After Tim Hortons Stabbing Granted Absolute Discharge
item.description: Kashane Daley, a 36-year-old man diagnosed with schizophrenia, was involved in a violent incident at a Toronto Tim Hortons on August 31, 2018. Daley, experiencing auditory and visual hallucinations, s...

item.title: Negotiations between Toronto Transit Commission and CUPE Local 2 extend beyond scheduled deadline
item.description: Negotiations between the Toronto Transit Commission (TTC) and the Canadian Union of Public Employees (CUPE) Local 2 have extended beyond their original deadline. These discussions are focused on labou...

item.title: Colin Jost reveals ‘SNL’ dismissed joke predicting Pete Hegseth’s Pulp Fiction Bible verse reading
item.description: During a recent appearance on The Tonight Show, Saturday Night Live writer and performer Colin Jost recounted how a joke he pitched about US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth inadvertently predicted a re...

item.title: Satwik and Chirag reach Thailand Open doubles final with hard-fought semifinal win
item.description: Indian badminton duo Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty demonstrated remarkable resilience and skill as they advanced to the men's doubles final at the Thailand Open Super 500 tournament in Ban...

item.title: Challenges in the 2026 Detroit Lions Schedule
item.description: The Detroit Lions' 2026 NFL schedule presents several challenges that may test the team’s endurance and strategy. One of the most notable difficulties is the cluster of late-season divisional road gam...

item.title: Karachi Court Remands Alleged Drug Suspect Anmol in Police Custody Until May 22
item.description: On Saturday, a judicial magistrate in Karachi remanded Anmol, also known as Pinky, in police custody until May 22 concerning a narcotics case. She was earlier arrested from her Karachi apartment over ...

item.title: All 22 Political Parties Submit Membership Registers Ahead of 2027 Elections, INEC Confirms
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) don announce say all the 22 registered political parties don submit their membership registers as e dey comply with the Electoral Act 2026 ahead of...

item.title: Military Rider Dies Following Accident at Royal Windsor Horse Show
item.description: A member of the King's Troop, Royal Horse Artillery, tragically died after falling from their horse at the Royal Windsor Horse Show on Friday evening. The incident occurred just after the soldier had ...

item.title: IFP puts forward Mlungisi Mabaso for Johannesburg mayor ahead of 2026 elections
item.description: The Inkatha Freedom Party (IFP) has officially announced Mlungisi Mabaso, the City of Johannesburg Human Settlements MMC, as its mayoral candidate for the upcoming 2026 local government elections. Thi...
